question: Using FY2025 Connectivity segment figures, calculate YoY growth in revenue, operating income, and Segment Adjusted EBITDA. What do the differential growth rates imply about operating leverage within Connectivity?
facts: Connectivity segment revenue was $11,387M in FY2025
Connectivity segment revenue was $7,599M in FY2024
Connectivity segment revenue grew 49.85% YoY
Connectivity segment operating income was $4,423M in FY2025
Connectivity segment operating income was $2,006M in FY2024
Connectivity segment operating income grew 120.49% YoY
Connectivity segment Adjusted EBITDA was $7,168M in FY2025
Connectivity segment Adjusted EBITDA was $3,849M in FY2024
Connectivity segment Adjusted EBITDA grew 86.23% YoY
Operating income grew at 2.4× the rate of revenue
Segment Adjusted EBITDA grew at 1.7× the rate of revenue
The differential growth rates demonstrate strong positive operating leverage within the Connectivity segment
Operating margin was 38.84% in FY2025
Operating margin was 26.40% in FY2024
Operating margin expanded 12.44 pp YoY
EBITDA margin was 62.95% in FY2025
EBITDA margin was 50.65% in FY2024
EBITDA margin expanded 12.30 pp YoY

question: Estimate implied monthly Starlink ARPU using Q1 2026 Connectivity revenue and disclosed subscriber counts. State all assumptions and identify what the filing’s subscriber and revenue definitions exclude from this ARPU estimate.
facts: Q1 2026 Connectivity Segment Revenue was $3,257 million
Consumer Revenue was $2,148 million in Q1 2026
Enterprise & Government Revenue was $1,109 million in Q1 2026
Products Revenue (all Connectivity) was $380 million in Q1 2026
Services Revenue was $2,877 million in Q1 2026
Starlink Subscribers as of March 31, 2026 were 10.3 million
Starlink Subscribers as of December 31, 2025 were 8.9 million
Average Subscribers during Q1 2026 were 9.6 million
Monthly ARPU using Total Connectivity revenue and end-of-period subscribers (10.3M) was $105.40
Monthly ARPU using Consumer Only revenue and end-of-period subscribers (10.3M) was $69.51
Monthly ARPU using Consumer Services Only revenue and end-of-period subscribers (10.3M) was $57.22
Monthly ARPU using Total Connectivity revenue and average subscribers (9.6M) was $113.09
Monthly ARPU using Consumer Only revenue and average subscribers (9.6M) was $74.58
Monthly ARPU using Consumer Services Only revenue and average subscribers (9.6M) was $61.39
Best-estimate blended monthly ARPU was ~$70–75
Estimated service-only monthly ARPU was ~$57–61 (upper bound; true figure likely higher)
The 10.3 million Starlink Subscribers map to the Consumer revenue category, not to Enterprise & Government revenue
The subscriber count of 10.3M is measured as of the last day of Q1 2026
Using the end-of-period subscriber figure understates ARPU relative to the true average revenue per subscriber during the quarter
Using the simple average of 9.6M subscribers provides a more accurate ARPU estimate
Consumer revenue includes both recurring service fees and one-time Starlink Kit (terminal) hardware sales
The filing does not break down Consumer revenue into Products vs. Services
The Consumer ARPU figures are blended (hardware + service)
The service-only ARPU cannot be precisely calculated from disclosed data
The Consumer Services Only ARPU estimate assumes 100% of the $380M Products revenue is attributable to Consumer
A Starlink Subscriber is defined as a unique Service Line assigned to a Starlink.com account that does not have a direct, negotiated agreement with the Starlink sales team
Managed enterprise customers with negotiated contracts are excluded from the subscriber count
Government customers and government entities are excluded from the subscriber count
Starlink Mobile end users (subscribers to MNO partner services) are excluded from the subscriber count
Multiple individual end users/physical persons sharing one Service Line are counted as one subscriber
The subscriber count is by Service Line, not by device
Connectivity segment revenue excludes Space Segment revenue
Connectivity segment revenue excludes Advertising Segment revenue
Connectivity segment revenue excludes AI Segment revenue
Enterprise & Government revenue ($1,109M) is included in Connectivity revenue but the corresponding customers are not counted in the 10.3M subscriber base
Starlink Mobile revenue (from MNO partnerships, included in Enterprise & Government) has no corresponding subscriber count in the 10.3M figure
The total Connectivity ARPU of ~$105–113/month significantly overstates the per-subscriber figure
The Consumer-only ARPU of ~$70–75/month is the more appropriate measure
The true service-only ARPU figure is likely somewhat higher than $57–61/month since some Products revenue is attributable to enterprise kit sales
The ~$70–75/month blended Consumer ARPU is the most reliable estimate derivable from disclosed data

question: Calculate AI and Connectivity segment capex intensity for FY2025 and Q1 2026. Compare quarter versus full-year intensity and assess what the change implies about the pace and stage of AI infrastructure buildout.
facts: AI Segment FY2025 Revenue was $3,201M
AI Segment Q1 2026 Revenue was $818M
AI Segment FY2025 Capex was $12,727M
AI Segment Q1 2026 Capex was $7,723M
Connectivity FY2025 Revenue was $11,387M
Connectivity Q1 2026 Revenue was $3,257M
Connectivity FY2025 Capex was $4,178M
Connectivity Q1 2026 Capex was $1,332M
AI segment FY2025 capex intensity was 398% (3.98x)
AI segment Q1 2026 capex intensity was 944% (9.44x)
Connectivity segment FY2025 capex intensity was 37% (0.37x)
Connectivity segment Q1 2026 capex intensity was 41% (0.41x)
AI Q1 2026 intensity of 944% is 2.37× the FY2025 full-year intensity of 398%
Connectivity Q1 2026 intensity of 41% is 1.11× the FY2025 full-year intensity of 37%
AI segment Q1 2025 capex intensity was 353% (3.53x)
Connectivity segment Q1 2025 capex intensity was 33% (0.33x)
The AI segment is in an early, aggressive infrastructure ramp, not a steady-state expansion
A capex intensity of 944% means the company is spending nearly $10 on infrastructure for every $1 of revenue generated
The company expects a "multi-year investment horizon" before achieving sustained positive AI Segment Adjusted EBITDA
The AI buildout trajectory from 353% (Q1 2025) → 398% (FY2025) → 944% (Q1 2026) shows the buildout is accelerating, not plateauing
AI capex is growing 120× faster than AI revenue on an annualized basis
The Connectivity segment's stable ~35–41% capex intensity reflects a business in the scaling/maturity phase
The AI segment's 944% intensity reflects a business in the capital-intensive buildout phase where investment precedes revenue by years
The commercial value proposition of frontier AI models remains "largely unproven"
There is no assurance that returns will justify the capital deployed
